headline: Transcript: The Wolf-Krugman Exchange — Power, plutocracy and political economy
summary: Martin Wolf talks to Paul Krugman
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of The Economics Show podcast episode: ‘The Wolf-Krugman Exchange — Power, plutocracy and political economy’

Martin WolfPaul. Hello, how are you?

Paul KrugmanI’m fine. It’s snowing heavily in New York, but I am not in New York, so I am doing fine.

Martin WolfSo today we’re recording an episode that’s a little different to the others in this series. We’ve talked about Trump’s America, we’ve talked about male employment in the US and we talked about China and Europe. But now we put the onus on you, the listener, to tell us what to talk about.

Paul KrugmanThat’s right. We’re going to answer listeners’ questions, some of which expand on subjects we’ve discussed, others that come a little more out of left field. And if I read correctly, a few out of right field as well.

Martin WolfWell, as you’ve said before, Paul, the Q&A is often the best part. So let’s get going.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

Hello, I’m Martin Wolf, chief economics commentator at the Financial Times.
